latest.title: Alaska Legislature Appearance & Op. Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5493G
Pantheon Resources PLC
02 June 2026
02 June 2026
Pantheon Resources plc
Appearance at Alaska Legislature Special Session and Operational Update
Pantheon Resources plc ("
Pantheon
" or the "
Company
"), the oil and gas company developing the Kodiak and Ahpun projects on Alaska's North Slope, is pleased to report that it was asked to join other North Slope producers before the Alaska Legislature's House Finance Committee ("the Committee") late last week, to provide an overview of the Company's projects and their potential to provide natural gas to the proposed natural gas pipeline being pursued by The Glenfarne Group and the State of Alaska (the "AK LNG Project").
The Legislature is currently in a Special Session focused exclusively on a tax relief package sponsored by the Governor to support the AK LNG Project. The Company joined ExxonMobil, ConocoPhillips, and Hilcorp as invited testimony from the proposed gas suppliers to the AK LNG Project.
Pantheon's session before the Committee included a discussion of the gas supply potential of its Kodiak and Ahpun projects, the advantageous low CO2 composition of its gas and the Company's ability and willingness to offer its gas at a relatively low price to improve the economics behind the AK LNG Project, particularly Phase 1 of the project which is intended to provide those in Southcentral Alaska with a reliable supply of natural gas at the lowest cost possible as they are facing a looming energy supply shortage.
A copy of the presentation can be found in the 'Presentations' section of the Company website. A recording of the session can be accessed through the following hyperlink
https://www.ktoo.org/gavel/video/?clientID=2147483647&eventID=2026051259
beginning at approximately 02:51:00 mark.
Operational Update
Pantheon continues to actively pursue all available strategic options to maximise value from its projects and confirms that the farm-out initiatives remain underway, with multiple parties engaged in the data room. As previously reported, such discussions are complex by nature, requiring extensive due diligence, and as such, a rapid outcome should not be expected, however progress is being made and the Company anticipates providing an update to the market before the end of summer.
- Ends -
For more information:

Olly Scott

latest.title: Operational Update and Change of Accounting Date
latest.content:
23 Apr 2026 11:12
RNS Number : 6915B
Pantheon Resources PLC
23 April 2026
23 April 2026
Pantheon Resources plc
Operational Update and Change of Accounting Reference Date
Pantheon Resources plc ("
Pantheon
" or the "
Company
"), the oil and gas company developing the Kodiak and Ahpun projects on Alaska's North Slope, is pleased to provide the following updates:
Operational Update
Since the AGM and Board changes in March, the Chairman and CEO of Pantheon have been almost exclusively focused on farm-out discussions for the Company. There is currently a number of different organisations of varying sizes in the data room with the majority remaining actively engaged and a number of additional companies continue to express interest in engaging.
These discussions are complex and a rapid outcome should not be expected but progress is being made. The Board is encouraged by the level of ongoing engagement, and the Company currently anticipates providing an update to the market before the end of the summer.
Change of Accounting Reference Date and Reporting Calendar
The Company announces that it has resolved to change the accounting reference date from 30 June to 31 December, with immediate effect.
As a result of the change, the Company will adhere to the following reporting timetable:
·
Publication of its unaudited interim financial results for the 6-month period ending 30 June 2026 by 30 September 2026
·
Publication of its audited results for the 18-month period to 31 December 2026 by 30 June 2027
·
Publication of its unaudited interim financial results for the 6-month period ending 30 June 2027 by 30 September 2027
·
Publication of its audited results for the 12-month period to 31 December 2027 by 30 June 2028
The Company believes this change will achieve a number of benefits including better alignment with internal planning and budgeting systems which operate on a calendar year basis, and to better align it with U.S. and U.K. tax regulatory timetables, improving efficiencies in tax compliance and in administration thereof.
Further information:
Pantheon Resources plc
Max Easley, CEO
Justin Hondris, SVP, Investor Relations

Joint Broker
)

latest.title: Granting RSU's and Blocklisting Application
latest.content:
13 Apr 2026 17:20
RNS Number : 2688A
Pantheon Resources PLC
13 April 2026
13 April 2026
Pantheon Resources plc
Granting Restricted Stock Units and Blocklisting Application
Pantheon Resources plc ("
Pantheon
" or the "
Company
"), the oil and gas company developing the Kodiak and Ahpun projects on Alaska's North Slope, announces the annual granting of restricted stock units ("
RSUs
") pursuant to Pantheon's Employee Stock Ownership Plan ("
ESOP
"), as announced in 2024 and 2025.
Grant of RSUs
In October 2024, Pantheon announced details of its replacement ESOP ("
2024 ESOP
") for all employees and a Long-Term Incentive Plan ("
LTIP
") for Executive Directors and certain officers of the Company. The Company retired its remaining share option-based incentive plan (originally adopted in 2009 and amended in 2014) with the ESOP which, where practicable, looks to follow the principles of the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
For further details on the ESOP, please refer to Note 23 within the Company's Consolidated Financial Statements for the financial year ended 30 June 2025, as included in the Company's Annual Report for that year.
Under the terms of the ESOP and as approved by the Company's Remuneration Committee, the Company has issued in aggregate 12,960,102 RSUs across all staff members (excluding non-executive directors). The number of RSUs awarded has been calculated using a price of £0.1160 being the closing share price at 8 April 2026 and the RSUs will vest equally in three annual tranches commencing 9 April 2027 and subject to certain conditions. Details of the RSUs granted to Persons Discharged with Managerial Responsibilities (PDMRs) are disclosed in the table below.
PDMR
2026 RSUs Granted
2026 RSUs as a % of Issued Share Capital
Max Easley
1,764,037
0.12%
Tralisa Maraj
1,363,119
0.09%
Erich Krumanocher
1,347,082
0.09%
Blocklisting Application
An application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for an additional headroom of 8,000,000 Ordinary Shares of £0.01 each (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") to be added to the existing block listing, as announced on 24 July 2025, (the "
2024 ESOP Blocklisting
"). It is expected that admission will become effective on or around 16 April 2026.
The New Ordinary Shares will be issued where necessary, in order to satisfy the issue of shares pursuant to the future exercise of previously granted LTIP options and the future vesting of previously granted RSU's (and possible RSU grants to future hires) under the ESOP. When issued, the New Ordinary Shares will be credited as fully paid and will rank pari passu in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares of £0.01 each in the capital of the Company ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company will provide bi-annual updates in respect of the 2024 ESOP Blocklisting.
The Company notes that the 2024 ESOP Blocklisting will continue to run in tandem to the Company's existing blocklisting, which will remain in place to satisfy the exercise of options under the Pantheon Resources Plc 2009 Discretionary Share Option Plan.

company_30s.what_they_do: Panoramic Resources is an exploration and development company focused on mining assets in Alaska. Its flagship project, Dubhe‑1, is a deep‑sea or underground deposit that the firm is working to evaluate and bring to production. The company also holds smaller sites like Kodiak and is actively seeking additional capital to fund drilling, environmental studies and regulatory approvals.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The resource sector remains a key driver of long‑term inflation and industrial demand. If Dubhe‑1 proves to contain commercially viable mineral or energy reserves, the company could become a high‑growth play. Its projects sit in a region with growing infrastructure investment and favorable mining policies, giving it a competitive edge over smaller, less‑capitalized peers.
company_30s.current_state: Panoramic is still in the pre‑production, high‑cash‑burn stage. The board has recently restructured, raised a $10 million bridge round, and announced a series of asset appraisals. The company is actively engaging regulators and investors, but remains cash‑constrained and has yet to produce any revenue.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings show a clear effort to shore up capital and stabilize governance: a new board, RSU grants, and a fresh $10 million raise are all aimed at extending the runway for drilling and regulatory work.

Operational updates on Dubhe‑1 and a positive appraisal from April suggest the asset is moving closer to a feasibility study, while the Alaska legislature appearance indicates the firm is courting state support and potential incentives.
Investor relations activity—webinars, presentations and AGM notices—points to a concerted push for transparency and to keep the market informed during a period of uncertainty.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are cheering the funding round and the asset appraisal, saying the company is “top‑up ready” and that a positive drill result could trigger a bounce.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A small group of skeptics point to the repeated board changes and the fact that the company has yet to deliver any tangible production milestones, warning that the cash burn could outpace the funding.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone is slightly shifting toward bullish as the funding announcement and the latest appraisal create a window for a potential rebound, but the overall chatter remains thin.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for PANR.L are flat, indicating a lack of public interest. This dead‑trend environment heightens the chance of a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the $10 million bridge round and the issuance of the proceeds to fund the next drilling phase.
Positive drilling results from Dubhe‑1 or the confirmation of a commercially viable resource in the April appraisal report.
A regulatory decision from the Alaska Legislature that unlocks permits or subsidies for the project.
A clear, high‑profile partnership or joint‑venture announcement that brings in technical expertise and additional capital.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm a bounce include: 1) a funding announcement with a realistic disbursement schedule; 2) a drill report that shows ore grades above the project’s break‑even threshold; 3) a regulatory approval letter; 4) a spike in trading volume above the 20‑day average coupled with a price move above the last support level.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery cycle. The APEX score of 9 suggests a tight compression, but without a clear catalyst the mean‑reversion remains uncertain. Confirmation is needed within the next 2–3 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the bridge round closes and Dubhe‑1 delivers a positive grade, the price could rebound to roughly half its peak level, yielding about 200% upside from the current trading range. The deep crash (likely >70%) provides a large asymmetric reward window, while the upcoming funding and asset data reduce the downside risk.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Should the funding fail, drilling results prove negative, or regulatory approval be delayed, the company could remain in a cash‑burn cycle that forces a further decline or even delisting. The worst‑case scenario is a total collapse of the asset value, wiping out the current market cap.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200% (from current price to half the historical high). Potential downside: ~70% (if the stock stays near its current level or falls further). The asymmetry favors a speculative long position with tight stop‑losses.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the most recent 20‑day support, with a stop 5–10% below that support. Allocate no more than 5% of the portfolio to this position, and monitor funding and drilling updates closely. Adjust the stop to breakeven once the price moves 15% higher.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until a funding announcement is made or a positive drill report is released. Watch for a volume spike of at least 30% above the 20‑day average as a confirmation signal.
playbook.if_passing: If no funding or drilling updates appear within 90 days, consider exiting or reducing exposure, as the company’s cash runway may become insufficient to sustain operations.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Panoramic Resources sits at the bottom of a deep, unsustainable crash; a timely funding round or positive asset data within the next few months is the only realistic path to a high‑reward rebound.
